description_long: This is a seated stone sculpture depicting a human figure, likely inspired by ancient Egyptian art. The figure wears a headdress reminiscent of a nemes, with detailed striations, and sits upright on a blocky throne with arms resting on the knees. The sculpture is robust and solid, with simplified anatomical features and a rectangular back support extending from the seat to the head. The surface is rough and pitted, showing signs of age or weathering, and the overall color is a pale, sandy beige. The back of the sculpture is less detailed, with a flat surface and visible tool marks, while the sides reveal the thickness of the seat and the figure's posture.
description: A pale, weathered stone sculpture of a seated human figure with a headdress, inspired by ancient Egyptian motifs, sitting on a blocky throne.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full figure, headdress, and both arms resting on the knees, with the face and torso clearly visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the headdress, the arm, and the blocky seat, with the back support visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat, less detailed back, the rectangular support, and the back of the seat.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the profile of the figure, arm, and seat, with the headdress and back support.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
